summary: No-ship for the absolute mutual-exclusion claim: there is still an unlink race in releaseLock that can remove a newly acquired stale-takeover lock, allowing a third session to acquire while the stale-takeover winner still believes it holds the lock.

findings.title: releaseLock can delete another session's newly acquired lock after a stale takeover
findings.recommendation: Make release atomic with holder verification at deletion time, e.g. use a compare-and-delete strategy such as writing/removing session-specific lock ownership, re-read immediately before unlink and only remove if unchanged, or replace the lock-file design with an atomic rename/CAS pattern. At minimum, do not unlink if the lock has expired; expired locks should be taken over by acquireLock rather than released by an old holder.
